content.thinking: There's a root-level node_modules with jest. Let me look for the root package.json and tsconfig.
All four files already exist and look complete. Let me verify the implementation is correct by running the tests.

content.content:
PASS .tmp/subscriptions/subscription-service.test.ts
  createSubscription
    ✓ AC1: creates a subscription with status 'active' for given userId and planId (1 ms)
    ✓ AC1: persists the subscription so it can be retrieved
    ✓ AC1: sets renewalDate to approximately now + 30 days (1 ms)
  getSubscription
    ✓ AC2: returns the subscription when it exists
    ✓ AC3: throws SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND when subscription does not exist (4 ms)
  cancelSubscription
    ✓ AC4: changes status to 'cancelled' and sets cancellationDate
    ✓ AC4: persisted subscription reflects cancelled status
    ✓ AC5: throws SUBSCRIPTION_ALREADY_CANCELLED when cancelling a cancelled subscription
    ✓ AC3 (via cancel): throws SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND for unknown id (1 ms)
  renewSubscription
    ✓ AC6: updates renewalDate to now + 30 days for an active subscription
    ✓ AC6: renew persists the updated renewalDate
    ✓ AC6: renew does not change status — stays 'active'
    ✓ AC7: throws CANNOT_RENEW_CANCELLED for a cancelled subscription (2 ms)
    ✓ AC3 (via renew): throws SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND for unknown id (1 ms)
  listSubscriptions
    ✓ AC8: returns all subscriptions (active and cancelled) for a user
    ✓ AC8: returns empty array when user has no subscriptions
    ✓ AC8: does not return subscriptions belonging to a different user
    ✓ AC8: reflects latest status after cancellation

Test Suites: 1 passed, 1 total
Tests:       18 passed, 18 total
Snapshots:   0 total
Time:        0.456 s, estimated 1 s
Ran all test suites matching /subscriptions\/subscription-service.test/i.
